claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my son Alex. He's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I have a few more questions:

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Is Alex in full-time education or training, or has he left education/training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's still at school - just doing his GCSEs at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Now I need to ask about your own circumstances. Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care (such as providing food, clothing, and making sure he attends school)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all his day-to-day care - making sure he's fed, clothed, getting to school, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Do you live in the UK? And is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Manchester. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise what you've told me:
- Alex is 15 years old
- He lives with you in Manchester (UK)
- He's in full-time education (doing his GCSEs)
- You're responsible for his day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✅ **Alex is under 16** – He's 15 years old, which means he qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes

✅ **You live in the UK** – You're based in Manchester

✅ **Alex lives with you** – And you're responsible for his day-to-day care (food, clothing, school attendance, etc.)

✅ **No one else is claiming** – There's no duplicate claim for Alex

You can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Once Alex turns 16, you'll be able to continue receiving Child Benefit as long as he stays in approved education or training (up until he turns 20).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 16 years old (aged 15), (2) He lives with the claimant who is responsible for his day-to-day care (providing food, clothing, and ensuring he attends school), (3) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), and (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 16 and in full-time education, there are no additional requirements to meet regarding approved education or training that would apply to children aged 16-19.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They meet the core requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 16 years old (aged 15), (2) He lives with the claimant who is responsible for his day-to-day care (providing food, clothing, and ensuring he attends school), (3) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), and (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 16 and in full-time education, there are no additional requirements to meet regarding approved education or training that would apply to children aged 16-19.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They meet the core requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.
